input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the bottom text input on Android, and the old `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach no longer works in .NET 10. This is a known behavioral change in .NET MAUI / Android.

In .NET 9 and earlier, you could use:
```csharp
App.Current.On<Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.Android>()
    .UseWindowSoftInputModeAdjust(WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize);
```

However, in newer versions of Android (API 30+), the `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` flag was deprecated by Google. .NET MAUI has been moving toward using the WindowInsets/Edge-to-Edge APIs instead.


For .NET 10, the better approach is to leverage the `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` behavior that MAUI introduced in .NET 8, which automatically adjusts content when the keyboard appears. Since `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was already being phased out on Android API 30+ starting in .NET 9, I should focus on either using the built-in keyboard manager or handling window insets manually. For .NET 10 specifically, `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` handles this automatically by default, but for chat layouts where the input sits at the bottom outside a scrollable area, I need to use a `Grid` with separate rows for the message list and input, then configure the Android manifest to handle keyboard insets properly.
